title: Self-employed Business, Professional, Commission, Farming, and Fishing Income: Capital cost allowance (CCA) rates - Canada.ca
content:
## Self-employed Business, Professional, Commission, Farming, and Fishing Income: Capital cost allowance (CCA) rates
Below you will find the more common depreciable properties that a business may use. The CCA rates appear at the end of the list. For more information on Classes 13 , 14, 34, and 43.1, and Part XVII of the Income Tax Act , call 1-800-959-5525 .
Depreciable property
Aircraft – Acquired before May 26, 1976
Aircraft – Acquired after May 25, 1976
Boats and component parts
Breakwaters – Cement or stone
Buildings and component parts – Wood, galvanized, or portable
Buildings and component parts – Other: Acquired after 1978 and before 1988 Footnote 1
Buildings and component parts – Other: Acquired after 1987
Buildings and component parts – Other: Fruit and vegetable storage (after Feb. 19, 1973 )
Casing, cribwork for water wells
Cleaners – grain or seed
Combines – Self-propelled
Computer equipment and systems software – Acquired before March 23, 2004
Computer equipment and systems software – Acquired after March 22, 2004
Computer equipment and systems software – Acquired after March 18, 2007
Computer equipment and systems software – Acquired after January 27, 2009 and before February 2011
Computer software (other than systems software)
Dams – Cement, stone, wood, or earth
Data network infrastructure equipment – Acquired after March 22, 2004
Dugouts, dikes, and lagoons
Electric-generating equipment – portable
Engines – Stationary
Forage harvesters – Drawn
Forage harvesters – Self-propelled
Graders – Fruit or vegetable
Grain-drying equipment
Grain-storage building – Wood, galvanized steel
Grain-storage building – Other
Greenhouses (all except as noted below)
Greenhouses of rigid frames covered with replaceable flexible plastic
Hay balers and stookers – Drawn
Hay balers and stookers – Self-propelled
Irrigation equipment – Overhead
Office equipment including photocopiers, fax machines
Passenger vehicles (see Chapter 4 )
Planters – All types
Power block – Purse seine
Radar or radio equipment – Acquired before May 26, 1976
Radar or radio equipment – Acquired after May 25, 1976
Roads or other surface areas – Paved or concrete
Swathers – Self-propelled
Tile or concrete drainage system – Acquired before 1965
Tools – Less than $500
Tools – $500 and more
Wharves – Cement, steel, or stone
Wind-energy conversion equipment – Acquired before February 22, 1994
Wind-energy conversion equipment – Acquired after February 21, 1994 Footnote 2
Zero-emission automotive equipment or vehicles (other than motor vehicles)
Zero-emission vehicles that would otherwise be in Class 10 or 10.1
Zero-emission vehicles that would otherwise be in Class 16

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
